display.purpose: Page titles only (league name, match ID). The one place Reaver appears.

title.purpose: Section header inside a Panel, uppercase, wide tracking.

body.purpose: Table cells, names, list rows. The workhorse text style.

label.purpose: Column headers, tab text, badges, round labels.

mono.purpose: Scores, win rates, ranks, durations; always tabular-nums.

shadows.name: tooltip-float
shadows.purpose: Ability tooltips and hovering detail panels that need to read as above the layout.

shadows.name: portrait-glow
shadows.purpose: Ambient separation for a hero/ability image sitting over busy background art.

shadows.name: active-row-inset
shadows.purpose: Marks the selected row in a roster or timeline list via a colored inset edge, not a lift.

shadows.name: text-legibility
shadows.purpose: Applied to text over background artwork or team logos as a contrast fix, not decoration.

components.name: Active Tab Pill
components.description: The selected tab in a sub-navigation row (league/player/leaderboard tabs).
components.css: .ds-tab { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: Radiance, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.1em; border-radius: 0; transition: background-color 0.15s, color 0.15s; } .ds-tab-active { background-color: #c9a94a; color: #0b0b0d; }

components.name: Inactive Tab Pill
components.css: .ds-tab { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: Radiance, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.1em; border-radius: 0; transition: background-color 0.15s, color 0.15s; } .ds-tab-inactive { background-color: transparent; color: #8a8474; } .ds-tab-inactive:hover { background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.05); color: #dcd6c8; }

components.name: Panel
components.description: The standard container: dark body, darker title strip, square corners, 1px border.
components.html:
Team Standings
Panel content goes here.
components.css: .ds-panel { background-color: rgba(12,11,14,0.72); border: 1px solid #24222a; border-radius: 0; color: #dcd6c8; font-family: Radiance, sans-serif; } .ds-panel-header { background-color: rgba(8,10,12,0.7); padding: 10px 16px; font-family: Reaver, serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 3px; border-bottom: 1px solid #24222a; } .ds-panel-body { padding: 16px; }

components.name: Private Badge
components.description: Marks a player profile that couldn't be publicly resolved. Muted, not alarming.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 6px; font-family: Radiance, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; border-radius: 0; border: 1px solid; } .ds-badge-private { color: #8a8474; border-color: #8a8474; background: transparent; }

components.name: Pro Badge
components.description: Marks a player known to be a professional competitor.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 6px; font-family: Radiance, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; border-radius: 0; border: 1px solid; } .ds-badge-pro { color: #c9a94a; border-color: rgba(201,169,74,0.5); background: transparent; }

components.name: Data Table Row
components.description: A row in a stats table: hairline top border, tabular numeric columns, optional hover wash when the row is a navigation target.
components.css: .ds-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 8px 12px; border-top: 1px solid #1c1810; font-family: Radiance, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; color: #dcd6c8; transition: background-color 0.15s; } .ds-row:hover { background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.04); cursor: pointer; } .ds-row-num { margin-left: auto; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; text-align: right; min-width: 56px; }

narrative.overview: DotaVault is built to feel like a panel that could have shipped inside the Dota 2 client itself, not a third-party website bolted on top of the game. It self-hosts Valve's actual web fonts (Radiance for UI text, Reaver for display headings) and borrows the game's own semantic vocabulary directly: Radiant green for wins, Dire red for losses, a muted gold for emphasis. Nothing here is a generic dashboard skin wearing esports colors; every choice traces back to something the audience already recognizes from hundreds of hours inside the game's own UI. The audience is competitive and hardcore players who arrive with a specific lookup already in mind. The design serves that: dense tables, compact rows, minimal chrome, zero onboarding copy. It explicitly rejects the generic SaaS/AI dashboard aesthetic (cream backgrounds, gradient text, card-grid-everything, tiny uppercase eyebrow labels above every section) in favor of the flat, near-black, sharp-edged surface Dota 2's own interface already uses.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black void background, never a light theme
Valve's own Radiance and Reaver fonts, self-hosted, not a lookalike
Sharp corners everywhere except circular avatars, matching Dota 2's own UI geometry
Color carries meaning (win/loss, radiant/dire, hero attribute) before it carries decoration
Data honesty: incomplete or approximate data is labeled as such, never hidden or faked

rules.name: The Named-Color Rule
rules.body: Every color in this system either comes from Dota 2's own UI/lore vocabulary (Radiant, Dire, Aegis Gold, the four attribute colors) or is a deliberately restrained neutral. If a new color is ever needed, ask first whether Dota 2 already has a name for it before inventing one.

rules.name: The Uppercase-Label Rule
rules.body: Any text acting as a label rather than content (section titles, column headers, tab text, badges) is uppercase with positive letter-spacing. Body content (names, values) is never uppercased.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Nothing gets a drop shadow just for being a card. Shadow is a functional answer to "this needs to read as above something else," never a default treatment for containers.

dos: Do name colors after Dota 2's own vocabulary first (Radiant, Dire, Aegis Gold, the attribute quartet) before inventing a new descriptive name.
Do keep corners square (0px radius) for every panel, card, button, and badge; reserve full rounding for player avatars only.
Do label incomplete or inferred data honestly (Player <id>, Team <id>, a Private badge, an Approximate round grouping disclaimer) rather than hiding the gap or fabricating a value.
Do use uppercase with positive letter-spacing for labels (section titles, column headers, tab text, badges); leave body content in normal case.
Do keep tables dense: compact row height, right-aligned tabular numbers, hairline dividers, no card-per-row treatment.
donts: Don't use a cream, off-white, or light theme background anywhere. The void background (#0b0b0d / #08080a) is non-negotiable.
Don't use gradient text, card-grid-everything layouts, or tiny uppercase eyebrow labels above every section, the generic SaaS/AI dashboard aesthetic this project explicitly rejects.
Don't add a drop shadow to a panel or card just because it's a container; shadow is reserved for tooltips, floating portraits, and active-row accents (see Elevation).
Don't round panel, card, or button corners. Square corners are the default; only player avatars break this rule.
Don't draw a bracket connector line (or any inferred relationship) across data the underlying source can't actually confirm; skip the line rather than mislead.
